businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robots, Megapack energy storage, and more. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin hit 20.1%, a 2-year high. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model. Key catalysts ahead: Cybercab production and Roadster unveiling in April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Still at extreme multiples despite -27% from 52-week high

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026 EPS estimate of $2.08. Over 4x higher than growth stock average of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: 2026 Delivery Guidance
keyMetrics.interpretation: Cut from 1.75M to 1.69M vehicles. Protest impact and European sales weakness now reflected in official guidance

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: 23-analyst consensus. +8% YoY from Q1 2025 (336,681) but -13% QoQ from Q4 2025 (418,227)

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: YTD -20%. Still larger than Toyota, VW, GM, Ford combined, but shrinking rapidly

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Extreme selling pressure but technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: Tesla gains 50%+ EV market share amid 28% industry slump (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: While the overall EV market plunged 28%, Tesla expanded its market share above 50%. Dominant brand power and charging infrastructure prove more valuable during downturns

growthDrivers.title: SpaceX IPO preparation rumors, Tesla valuation catalyst (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: SpaceX IPO rumors spreading. Could serve as indirect catalyst for Tesla investors and trigger revaluation of the entire Musk ecosystem

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed Optimus3 reduction gear and precision hand design. Targeting 1M unit production rate by end of 2026. Trefis values robot business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster April unveil
growthDrivers.description: Steerless autonomous robotaxi Cybercab production and next-gen Roadster unveiling both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in one month

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reaffirmed $600 target, calling Terafab semiconductor facility a 'game changer'. AI5 chip limited production starting late 2026

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD investigation escalated, regulatory risk rising (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA escalated its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warns this could break the '$1 trillion AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles FSD data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Competitors cannot replicate this scale of autonomous driving training data in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Even Ford, GM use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fanbase generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Rapidly catching Tesla on price competitiveness

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas. Pilot in Austin, Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in autonomous commercialization. 200K+ paid rides per week. Expanding robotaxi service

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Ioniq, EV6 expanding global EV share. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Tesla cuts 2026 delivery guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M vehicles
recentNews.significance: ARK Invest trimmed Tesla positions. TipRanks reports Tesla lowered 2026 delivery outlook from 1.75M to 1.69M. Protest impact and European weakness are the primary drivers

recentNews.headline: Federal regulators probe Tesla's camera-only FSD system
recentNews.significance: Investigation focused on FSD performance in low-visibility conditions (fog, dust). Key risk to autonomous driving valuation

recentNews.headline: Cathie Wood/ARK trims Tesla, part of $84M tech selloff
recentNews.significance: ARK simultaneously sold TSLA, NVDA, META, AMD. ARK trimming Tesla is psychologically significant as they've been the most prominent bull

recentNews.headline: 'No Kings' protests draw millions worldwide, 200+ target Tesla
recentNews.significance: Millions participated in thousands of protests globally. Tesla directly targeted in 200+ anti-Tesla protests due to Musk's DOGE political activity

recentNews.headline: Tesla gains 50%+ market share in brutal 28% EV sales slump
recentNews.significance: Tesla expanded market share even as total EV market collapsed. Proves strength consolidation during downturns

analystOpinions.notableComment: HSBC $119 low vs Stifel $508, Wedbush $600. Extreme polarization persists. ARK/Cathie Wood trimming is a cautionary short-term signal

risks.title: 2026 delivery guidance cut to 1.69M, growth slowdown confirmed
risks.description: Lowered from 1.75M to 1.69M. Protest impact and European weakness are now in the numbers. Even ARK/Cathie Wood trimmed positions, shaking the bull case's most prominent advocate

risks.title: 'No Kings' protests spreading to small towns
risks.description: Reuters reports small-town protest participation up 40% from first mobilization. European registrations already down 17%. Brand damage expanding from metro to nationwide structural crisis

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, RSI 33 oversold
risks.description: Trading below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). RSI 33 nearing oversold threshold. Five consecutive down days

risks.title: HSBC $119 target cut and ARK selling simultaneously
risks.description: HSBC re-cut to $119 while ARK sold. Bear and bull camp both acting negatively in an unusual convergence

risks.title: FSD regulatory and safety risk intensifying
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalated. Camera-only system probe launched. Trefis warns the '$1 trillion AI thesis could break'

risks.title: Extreme valuation risk
risks.description: TTM P/E 335x is 14.5x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Delivery guidance cut makes valuation justification even harder

buyReasons.title: Market share expansion proves competitive strength in downturn
buyReasons.rationale: 50%+ market share gained during a 28% EV market crash. The strong get stronger in tough times

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration (Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Netherlands FSD approval, Q1 earnings (4/21) all concentrated in April for potential momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 33, entering technical oversold territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI near 30 oversold threshold. Down 27% from 52-week high. Technical bounce probability increasing

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus robot as long-term revaluation catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production start could justify Tesla's long-term valuation as a Physical AI platform

overallOpinion: Tesla cut its 2026 delivery guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M. Protest impact and European weakness are now hitting actual numbers. Even Cathie Wood's ARK trimmed Tesla, shaking the bull camp's most prominent advocate
HSBC cut to $119 and ARK sold on the same day. Bear and bull camps both moving negatively is an unusual convergence. The 5x gap between Stifel $508 and HSBC $119 shows extreme market division

April catalysts are concentrated within 3 weeks: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, 4/21 earnings. The 50%+ market share in a -28% EV market proves resilience, but the guidance cut has cracked that armor
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below both 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). The guidance cut has lowered expectations for 4/21 earnings. Hold, waiting for confirmation is now more important than ever

investmentVerdict.summary: The delivery guidance cut to 1.69M and ARK selling reinforce the Hold stance. RSI 33 oversold and April catalysts provide bounce potential, but protest impact hitting actual guidance confirms the risk is real. Wait for 4/21 earnings to assess the guidance cut's impact

sources.name: TipRanks - ARK selling and delivery guidance cut
sources.description: Tesla 2026 delivery outlook cut from 1.75M to 1.69M, ARK $84M tech selloff

sources.name: Simply Wall St - FSD autonomy probe
sources.description: Federal regulators launch probe into FSD camera-only system

sources.name: Reuters - No Kings protests, small-town participation up 40%
sources.description: Small-town protest participation up 40% from first mobilization

sources.name: TIKR - HSBC $119 target cut
sources.description: HSBC Reduce rating, target cut from $133 to $119

sources.name: InsideEVs - Tesla gains 50%+ share in 28% EV slump
sources.description: Tesla expands market share amid EV market collapse

sources.name: Benzinga - Tesla wins in brutal EV slump
sources.description: EV sales down 28% but Tesla market share expands

2026 delivery guidance cut from 1.75M to 1.69M.
Cathie Wood's ARK is trimming positions.

thread:

threads.hook: TSLA: Delivery guidance cut to 1.69M, even ARK is trimming

April: Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings. 50%+ EV market share is solid, but guidance cut is heavy.

RSI 33 oversold. Hold. 4/21 earnings just became more critical.

witty.hook: Even ARK is trimming. When the last bull wobbles, who's left to buy?
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%)

Cathie Wood trimmed Tesla.
Delivery guidance: 1.75M down to 1.69M.

When the most famous Tesla bull trims,
who's left holding the line?

1/4
thread:
HSBC: $119. ARK: selling.
Same day, same direction.

When bears and bulls agree,
it's usually not good news.

2/4

50%+ EV share in a -28% market.
Impressive, truly.

But if you're cutting guidance,
that armor has a crack.

3/4

RSI 33. April is loaded with catalysts.

Could bounce. But right now,
'wait' beats 'buy.' Hold.

4/4

witty.hook: Even ARK trimmed. When the last bull retreats, who buys?
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%)

Cathie Wood trimmed. HSBC at $119 and ARK selling on the same day. Bears and bulls agreeing is rare, and usually bad.

Guidance: 1.75M to 1.69M. Protests went from chants to spreadsheets.

50%+ EV share. The armor is strong but cracking. April catalysts could patch it or break it.

RSI 33. Hold beats buy right now.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, ARK selling and guidance cut. We get it
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83(-2.76%), 27% from highs.

Cathie Wood trimmed and guidance dropped to 1.69M. When your biggest cheerleader goes quiet, it's unsettling.

But 50%+ EV share in a -28% market proves the company is still standing. April is packed with catalysts.

RSI 33. Patience over panic. Wait for 4/21 earnings.

Hold. Confirmation first.
